generated: Contrast-enhanced images were taken in the axial plane with a section thickness of 2.5 mm. Trachea, both main bronchi are open. Mediastinal evaluation reveals prominent lymphadenopathy with a 19 mm subcarinal node. Heart contour is enlarged suggesting mild cardiomegaly. Thoracic aorta shows ectasia measuring 43 mm in diameter. Pericardial effusion measuring 8 mm in thickness is observed. Thoracic esophageal calibration was normal and no significant tumoral wall thickening was detected. When examined in the lung parenchyma window; diffuse bilateral ground-glass attenuation is present in the lower zones with interlobular septal thickening, compatible with fibrotic interstitial lung disease. A 25 mm dense consolidation occupies the lingula. No discrete pulmonary nodules were identified. Moderate bilateral pleural effusion with compressive atelectasis at both lung bases is noted. The spleen is enlarged at 15 cm. A 28 mm exophytic renal cyst arising from the left kidney is noted. The pancreas appears atrophic. Degenerative disc disease with compression fracture at T12 showing 30% height reduction. Sclerotic focus in the right iliac bone.
ground_truth: Non-contrast images were taken in the axial plane with a section thickness of 1.5 mm.,Trachea, both main bronchi are open. Mediastinal main vascular structures, heart contour, size are normal. Thoracic aorta diameter is normal. Pericardial effusion-thickening was not observed. Thoracic esophageal calibration was normal and no significant tumoral wall thickening was detected. No enlarged lymph nodes in prevascular, pre-paratracheal, subcarinal or bilateral hilar-axillary pathological dimensions were detected. When examined in the lung parenchyma window; A few millimetric nonspecific nodules and mild recessions are observed in the upper lobe and lower lobe of the right lung. Aeration of both lung parenchyma is normal and no infiltrative lesion is detected in the lung parenchyma. Pleural effusion-thickening was not detected. Upper abdominal organs included in the sections are normal. No space-occupying lesion was detected in the liver that entered the cross-sectional area. Bilateral adrenal glands were normal and no space-occupying lesion was detected. Bone structures in the study area are natural. Vertebral corpus heights are preserved., A few millimetric nonspecific nodules and slight recessions in the upper lobe and lower lobe of the right lung.

generated: Contrast-enhanced sections were acquired in the axial plane with 3 mm slice thickness. No soft tissue mass was identified in the anterior shoulder region. On the right, thyroid glands are symmetric with homogeneous parenchyma and normal dimensions. The central airways are patent without intraluminal obstruction. Thoracic vascular calibration appears within normal range. Ascending aorta measures 38 mm, mildly dilated. Cardiac silhouette dimensions are unremarkable. Pericardial fluid stripe measuring 10 mm is identified along the inferior cardiac margin. The distal esophagus is unremarkable. Sliding type hiatal hernia was observed. A 12 mm lymph node in the right hilum is suspicious for pathological enlargement. No axillary lymphadenopathy was identified bilaterally. In the pulmonary parenchyma; bilateral lung fields are clear with no peribronchial wall thickening. No parenchymal nodules or mass lesions were identified in either lung. Moderate left-sided pleural fluid collection is present with passive atelectasis. A 14 mm hypodense lesion in hepatic segment VI and hepatomegaly measuring 19 cm are noted. Right adrenal gland shows a 12 mm nodular lesion; left adrenal appears unremarkable. Multiple lytic osseous lesions are detected in the thoracic spine and bilateral ribs, suspicious for metastatic disease. Diffuse right breast cutaneous thickening is observed.
ground_truth: 1.5 mm thick non-contrast sections were taken in the axial plane. As far as can be seen; A stable soft tissue mass of approximately 5x4x5. On the right, both thyroid glands have increased in size and their parenchyma is heterogeneous. US control is recommended. Trachea and lumen of both main bronchi are open. No occlusive pathology was detected in the trachea and lumen of both main bronchi. Calibration of thoracic main vascular structures is natural. No dilatation was detected in the thoracic aorta. Heart contour size is natural. Pericardial thickening-effusion was not detected. Thoracic esophagus calibration was normal and no significant pathological wall thickening was detected. Sliding type hiatal hernia was observed. No lymph node was detected in mediastinal and bilateral hilar pathological size and appearance. Heterogeneous density increases were observed in the subcutaneous fatty planes in the left axillary region (secondary to post-treatment?). When examined in the lung parenchyma window; Contour irregularities were observed in the pleura in the upper lobe of the left lung. Posttreatment was evaluated in favor of secondary changes. Bilateral peribronchial thickenings were observed. No pleural effusion was detected. Multiple parenchymal nodules of stable size and number, which were initially evaluated in favor of metastasis, were observed in both lungs. Upper abdominal sections entering the examination area are natural. Bilateral adrenal gland calibration was normal and no space-occupying lesion was detected. No lytic-destructive lesion was detected in bone structures.Metastatic breast ca.  Multiple parenchymal nodules in both lungs evaluated in favor of metastasis, post-RT sequelae changes in the upper lobe of the left lung, and bilateral peribronchial thickenings.  Stable mass lesion in anterior left shoulder.  Hiatal hernia.  Thickening of the left breast skin.

generated: Contrast-enhanced helical CT with 3 mm collimation in axial acquisition. The airway is patent at all levels; trachea is midline. Mediastinal fat planes are well-preserved and the contrast study allows adequate assessment. Cardiovascular silhouette is within normal limits. No pericardial abnormality is detected. The distal esophagus appears unremarkable without tumoral thickening; no hernia at the gastroesophageal junction. Prevascular and paratracheal nodal stations are clear; however a 16 mm right hilar lymph node is identified, suspicious for reactive or neoplastic etiology. When examined in the lung parenchyma window; a spiculated 18 mm right upper lobe mass abutting the oblique fissure is observed, concerning for primary bronchogenic carcinoma. PET-CT correlation is advised. Scattered sub-centimeter bilateral pulmonary nodules are present, largest 9 mm in the left lower lobe. No ground-glass density or crazy-paving pattern to suggest viral pneumonia. A well-circumscribed 35 mm left lower lobe mass is present. Bilateral pleural fluid accumulation is noted, small on the right and moderate on the left. The gallbladder is distended with dependent calculi. Hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal abnormality. A 13 mm left adrenal nodule warrants dedicated adrenal protocol CT; the right gland is unremarkable. Diffuse osteopenia is present without discrete lytic or blastic lesion. Vertebral alignment and body heights are maintained throughout.
ground_truth: Non-contrast images were taken in the axial plane with a section thickness of 1.5 mm.Trachea was in the midline of both main bronchi and no obstructive pathology was observed in the lumen. The mediastinum could not be evaluated optimally in the non-contrast examination. As far as can be seen; mediastinal main vascular structures, heart contour, size are normal. Pericardial effusion-thickening was not observed. Thoracic esophagus calibration was normal and no significant tumoral wall thickening was detected. Sliding type hiatal hernia was observed at the lower end of the esophagus. No enlarged lymph nodes in prevascular, pre-paratracheal, subcarinal or bilateral hilar-axillary pathological dimensions were detected. When examined in the lung parenchyma window; On the right, nodular images with a size of 6x2.5 mm, superposed on the major fissure, were observed and were initially evaluated in favor of the intrapulmonary lymph node. Focal nodular opacity with vascular enlargement is observed in the right lung middle lobe adjacent to the major fissure, in the left lung lower lobe basal segment and lower lobe superior segment, and in the right lung lower lobe mediobasal segment, and it is suspicious for ultra-early Covid-19 pneumonia. It is recommended to be evaluated together with clinical and laboratory. No mass lesion with distinguishable borders was detected in both lungs. As far as can be seen within the sections; upper abdominal organs are normal. No space-occupying lesion was detected in the liver that entered the cross-sectional area. Bilateral adrenal glands were normal and no space-occupying lesion was detected. Bone structures in the study area are natural. Vertebral corpus heights are preserved.Hiatal hernia. Appearance in both lungs that may be compatible with ultra-early Covid-19 pneumonia; It is recommended to be evaluated together with clinical and laboratory.
